LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS UNVERIFIED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES IN THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO APRIL 19, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, TO BE HEARD CONCURRENTLY WITH THE DEMURRER FILED BY FORD. COUNSEL FOR FORD IS ADMONISHED THAT THE LOCAL RULES REQUIRE THAT A PARTY FILING A MOTION CHALLENGING A PLEADING IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT A COURTESY COPY OF THAT PLEADING TO DEPARTMENT 302. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS ADMONISHED THAT THE LOCAL RULES REQUIRE THAT COURTESY COPIES OF ALL FILED PAPERS MUST BE SUBMITTED TO DEPARTMENT 302. MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANYS DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFFS UNVERIFIED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 60198582) FILED BY DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO APRIL 19, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, DUE TO THE PARTIES' FAILURE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE DEMURRER AS REQUIRED BY CCP 430.41. THIS SHOULD GIVE THE PARTIES SUFFICIENT TIME TO AGREE TO A RESOLUTION WHICH PRECLUDES THE COURT FROM HAVING TO RULE ON THE SAME ISSUE THAT THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY RULED. THE COURT IS AT A LOSS TO UNDERSTAND WHY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL CONTINUES TO INCLUDE THE SAME DEFICIENT LANGUAGE IN THE COMPLAINTS THEY HAVE FILED IN THIS COUNTY AFTER THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY RULED THAT THE LANGUAGE IS DEFICIENT. (SEE DEMURRER ORDER IN PETREE V. FORD, CASE NUMBER 553491 MEMORIALIZING THE ORAL RULING MADE AT THE HEARING ON NOVEMBER 16, 2016). THE PARTIES ARE WASTING EACH OTHER'S AND THE COURT'S TIME BY FILING AND OPPOSING DEMURRERS ON THE SAME GROUNDS THAT THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY RULED. PLEASE REFRAIN FROM DOING SO IN THE FUTURE. COUNSEL FOR FORD IS ADMONISHED THAT THE LOCAL RULES REQUIRE THAT A PARTY FILING A MOTION CHALLENGING A PLEADING IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT A COURTESY COPY OF THAT PLEADING TO DEPARTMENT 302. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS ADMONISHED THAT THE LOCAL RULES REQUIRE THAT COURTESY COPIES OF ALL FILED PAPERS MUST BE SUBMITTED TO DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

Defendant Ford Motor Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Unverified Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Continued To Apr-19-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Unverified Complaint is continued on the court's own motion to April 19, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, to be heard concurrently with the demurrer filed by Ford. Counsel for Ford is admonished that the Local Rules require that a party filing a motion challenging a pleading is required to submit a courtesy copy of that pleading to department 302. Counsel for the plaintiff is admonished that the Local Rules require that courtesy copies of all filed papers must be submitted to department 302. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Continued To Apr-19-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued on the court's own motion to April 19, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, due to the parties' failure to meet and confer regarding the issues raised by the demurrer as required by CCP 430.41. This should give the parties sufficient time to agree to a resolution which precludes the court from having to rule on the same issue that the court has previously ruled. The court is at a loss to understand why plaintiff's counsel continues to include the same deficient language in the complaints they have filed in this county after the court has previously ruled that the language is deficient. (See demurrer order in Petree v. Ford, case number 553491 memorializing the oral ruling made at the hearing on November 16, 2016). The parties are wasting each other's and the court's time by filing and opposing demurrers on the same grounds that the court has previously ruled. Please refrain from doing so in the future. Counsel for Ford is admonished that the Local Rules require that a party filing a motion challenging a pleading is required to submit a courtesy copy of that pleading to department 302. Counsel for the plaintiff is admonished that the Local Rules require that courtesy copies of all filed papers must be submitted to department 302.
